agent.name: performance-cost-auditor
agent.role: Audit and report issues for this suite.

findings.title: Unoptimized SELECT * Queries
findings.description: Multiple Supabase queries use .select('*') to fetch all columns. This wastes bandwidth and memory, especially for tables with large text or JSONB fields like projects and task_prompts.
findings.impact: Multiple Supabase queries use .select('*') to fetch all columns. This wastes bandwidth and memory, especially for tables with large text or JSONB fields like projects and task_prompts.

suggested_fix.approach: Explicitly specify only the required columns in the .select() chain for better database performance.

findings.title: Missing staleTime in React Query Hooks
findings.description: Almost all project hooks (useProjects, useProjectTasks, etc.) omit the staleTime configuration. With the default of 0ms, this triggers redundant network requests on every window focus, component remount, and network reconnect.

proof.actual: Missing staleTime property in useQuery config object

proof_hooks.actual: Missing staleTime property in useQuery config object

findings.impact: Almost all project hooks (useProjects, useProjectTasks, etc.) omit the staleTime configuration. With the default of 0ms, this triggers redundant network requests on every window focus, component remount, and network reconnect.

suggested_fix.approach: Configure an appropriate staleTime (e.g., 1-5 minutes) for data that does not change frequently, to minimize redundant API/DB calls.

findings.title: Missing Code Splitting for Large Libraries
findings.description: The application relies on heavy dependencies (monaco-editor, @xyflow/react, html2canvas, jspdf, recharts) but lacks any React.lazy() or dynamic import() code-splitting boundaries. This forces all users to download the full weight of these libraries upfront, severely impacting initial load performance.

proof.expected: Dynamic chunks for editor and flow diagram components
proof.actual: No dynamic imports () found in src code; large dependencies are statically compiled to the main bundle.

proof_hooks.summary: Dynamic chunks for editor and flow diagram components
proof_hooks.expected: Dynamic chunks for editor and flow diagram components
proof_hooks.actual: No dynamic imports () found in src code; large dependencies are statically compiled to the main bundle.

findings.impact: The application relies on heavy dependencies (monaco-editor, @xyflow/react, html2canvas, jspdf, recharts) but lacks any React.lazy() or dynamic import() code-splitting boundaries. This forces all users to download the full weight of these libraries upfront, severely impacting initial load performance.

suggested_fix.approach: Wrap heavy, route-specific components in React.lazy() and Suspense boundaries to lazily load expensive libraries, and configure Vite manualChunks where appropriate.

next_actions.action: Audit SELECT * queries in adapter files and replace with specific column whitelists.
Add staleTime configuration to all React Query useQuery hooks.
Implement React.lazy() for route-level code splitting, especially for the IDE/Editor and Flow routes.
